Title: Xanthomonas oryzae Orphan Response Regulator EmvR Is Involved in Virulence, Extracellular Polysaccharide Production and Cell Motility

Common_Genes.Subcellular_Localization: inner membrane-associated / cytoplasmic pilus assembly motor
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Required for twitching motility and negatively associated with spreading motility; its ATPase activity is repressed by EmvR
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: protein purification and ATPase assay; deletion mutant; complementation; bacterial two-hybrid; pull-down assay
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. PilB showed ATPase activity in vitro. 2. EmvR physically interacted with PilB and reduced its ATPase activity by about 40.4%. 3. Deletion of pilB almost abolished twitching motility and greatly increased spreading motility. 4. Complementation restored wild-type motility.
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Loss of PilB severely impairs T4P-dependent twitching, likely compromising host surface colonization
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential antibacterial target to block T4P function and early host invasion of Xoc
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: PilB is a motility factor rather than a direct EPS biosynthetic enzyme, but it is central to the EmvR-controlled phenotype in this paper.

Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: protein activity modulation; likely phosphorylation-dependent signaling; repression of target ATPase activity
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Key controller of extracellular polysaccharide production and type IV pilus-dependent motility; required for full virulence during early stomatal invasion
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: EPS production; indirect control of type IV pilus biogenesis-associated surface behavior
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: likely signals sensed by ColS_XOCgx_4036; unknown environmental or emergency stimulus
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: response regulator receiver (REC) domain-containing single-domain RR
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: likely transient signaling complex with ColS_XOCgx_4036 and target interaction with PilB
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes EPS production and spreading motility, represses twitching motility, and supports virulence in spray inoculation
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: in-frame deletion mutant; complementation; overexpression; RT-qPCR; co-IP coupled LC-MS/MS; bacterial two-hybrid; pull-down assay; ATPase assay; virulence assay; site-directed mutagenesis; in vitro phosphorylation assay
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Deletion of emvR reduced spreading motility and spray-inoculation virulence, while increasing twitching motility. 2. Overexpression of emvR increased EPS production and spreading motility but reduced twitching motility. 3. EmvR physically interacted with PilB and repressed its ATPase activity as a non-competitive inhibitor. 4. EmvR also interacted with sensor kinase ColS_XOCgx_4036 and genetically functioned downstream of ColS_XOCgx_4036 in motility control. 5. Asp-15, Asp-59, and Thr-87 were essential for regulatory function, and Asp-59 was the phosphorylation site required for activity.
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Deletion lowers virulence during natural-like spray infection; overexpression alters motility balance and may attenuate twitching/T4P activity
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential antimicrobial target to reduce Xoc virulence and EPS-associated colonization by interfering with EmvR-PilB or EmvR-ColS signaling
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: EmvR affected virulence in spraying but not infiltration assays, indicating a role in early stomatal entry rather than later in planta growth. It is proposed to act in a branched one-to-two TCS with ColS_XOCgx_4036 and ColR_XOCgx_4037.
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: spray versus infiltration inoculation; EPS-inducing sucrose media

Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: likely transcriptional regulation downstream of sensor histidine kinase ColS_XOCgx_4036
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Positive regulator of EPS production and controller of motility phenotypes
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: ColS_XOCgx_4036-mediated two-component signaling; unknown environmental cue
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: changes expression of genes directly or indirectly involved in motility and EPS production
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: two-component response regulator with REC domain and DNA-binding domain
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes EPS production and spreading motility and represses twitching motility
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: in-frame deletion mutant; complementation; cross-complementation with emvR overexpression; EPS assay; motility assay
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Deletion of colR_XOCgx_4037 reduced EPS production by 27.7%. 2. Deletion also weakened spreading motility and enhanced twitching motility. 3. Complementation restored wild-type phenotypes. 4. Overexpression of emvR in the colR mutant restored EPS and spreading, placing EmvR as a compensating downstream effector branch for some phenotypes.
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Loss of ColR decreases EPS and alters motility balance
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Candidate antibacterial target for limiting EPS-associated colonization and motility in Xoc
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Direct downstream genes were not identified experimentally in this paper.

Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: two-component phosphotransfer signaling; physical interaction with EmvR
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Controller of motility-related branch and upstream activator/modulator of EmvR-dependent signaling
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: motility-associated regulation; indirect effect on T4P biogenesis
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: unknown environmental cue or emergency stimulus
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: acts via ColR_XOCgx_4037 and likely via EmvR to influence motility-related outputs
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: canonical ColS/ColR TCS and proposed branched TCS with EmvR
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes spreading motility and represses twitching motility, with no significant effect on EPS production in this study
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: in-frame deletion mutant; complementation; bacterial two-hybrid; pull-down assay; cross-complementation with emvR overexpression; motility assay; EPS assay
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Deletion of colS_XOCgx_4036 weakened spreading motility and enhanced twitching motility. 2. Unlike ColR_XOCgx_4037, deletion did not significantly affect EPS production. 3. EmvR physically interacted with ColS_XOCgx_4036. 4. Overexpression of emvR in the colS mutant restored EPS production and spreading motility, supporting a downstream role for EmvR in the ColS branch.
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Loss perturbs motility behavior likely affecting infection-related surface colonization
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential target for disrupting surface motility and host entry of Xoc
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Phosphotransfer from ColS_XOCgx_4036 to EmvR was proposed but not directly detected under assay conditions.
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: unknown environmental cue; EPS-inducing sucrose media
